message:
Add reusable workflow pattern for major releases

- Add workflow_call trigger to release.yaml for reusability
- Create release-major.yaml shim that dispatches to main workflow
- Update input references to use inputs.X (works for both triggers)

Major releases now use a separate workflow that calls the main one,
keeping all logic in one place while providing a deliberate path for
major version bumps.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+name: Release (Major)
+
+on:
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      description:
+        description: |
+          Description of the release
+
+          The description of the release should be 1-2 sentences long, must be
+          written in active voice, and should clearly communicate the theme of
+          the release to the user. Use full sentences and avoid abbreviations.
+        required: true
+        type: string
+
+jobs:
+  release:
+    uses: ./.github/workflows/release.yaml
+    with:
+      bump: major
+      description: ${{ inputs.description }}
+    secrets: inherit

@@ -19,6 +19,16 @@ on:
           the release to the user. Use full sentences and avoid abbreviations.
         required: true
         type: string
+  workflow_call:
+    inputs:
+      bump:
+        description: Version bump type (minor, patch, or major)
+        required: true
+        type: string
+      description:
+        description: Description of the release
+        required: true
+        type: string
 
 jobs:
   create:
@@ -95,10 +105,10 @@ jobs:
       - name: Create release
         id: release
         env:
-          RELEASE_DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.inputs.description }}
+          RELEASE_DESCRIPTION: ${{ inputs.description }}
         run: |
           VERSION=$(uvx tenzir-changelog release create \
-            --${{ github.event.inputs.bump }} \
+            --${{ inputs.bump }} \
             --intro "$RELEASE_DESCRIPTION" \
             --yes)
           echo "version=${VERSION}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
